request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-19 [A] CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law — CFTC suing Minnesota to block state crypto law suggests federal enforcement can resolve jurisdictional disputes, reducing pressure to enact CLARITY Act.
- 2026-05-18 [background] Rescission of Policy Regarding Denials in Settlements of Enforcement Actions — Securities and Exchange Commission — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-05-08 [background] Self-Regulatory Organizations; The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC; Notice of Filing of Proposed Rule Change, as Modified by Amendment No. 1 Thereto, to List and Trade Shares of iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF under Nasdaq Rule 5711(d) (CommodityBased Trust... — Securities and Exchange Commission — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-05-06 [background] CDNA - Amendments to rules on Insider definition, jurisdiction… - 05/06/2026 — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - Restructure the Disciplinary Panel and Appeals Committee from standing committees to ad hoc committees convened on an as-needed basis. - GMNI — Internal CFTC committee restructuring is administrative and unlikely to affect congressional passage or presidential signing of H.R.3633.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - Block Trade Rebate Program - KEX — CFTC's block-trade rebate action for Kalshi is an administrative market move unrelated to passage of H.R.3633, so it gives no clear signal.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Non-Crypto Market Maker Program - 04/16/2026 — CFTC's Coinbase market-maker program shows regulator activity on crypto markets but doesn't materially change likelihood of H.R.3633 passage.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Modifications to trading hours… - 04/16/2026 — Routine CFTC action on Coinbase trading hours is background regulatory activity and does not materially change odds that H.R.3633 will be enacted.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Modifications to implement hard limits… - 04/16/2026 — CFTC modifications on derivatives/position limits are background regulatory activity; they provide context but do not change odds of H.R.3633 becoming law.
- 2026-04-13 [A] Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities — SEC staff push to treat crypto interfaces as broker‑dealers raises pressure for Congress to pass the CLARITY Act shifting crypto jurisdiction to CFTC.
- 2026-04-08 [background] Treasury Proposes Rule to Implement the GENIUS Act’s Requirements to Counter Illicit Finance — FinCEN's AML rulemaking implements the GENIUS Act (illicit‑finance rules), not the CLARITY Act's SEC/CFTC jurisdiction, so it has no direct effect on H.R.3633's passage.
- 2026-04-01 [background] BTNL Rules 501 and 504 Update [1] — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-03-23 [A] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets (PDF) — SEC Federal Register action asserting securities jurisdiction over crypto reduces momentum for a CFTC-focused CLARITY Act, lowering its odds.
- 2026-03-23 [A] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets — SEC interpretive action on crypto highlights regulatory conflict and could increase Congressional pressure to pass the CLARITY Act, raising YES probability.
- 2026-03-18 [A] Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets Corporation Finance — SEC guidance asserting broad securities coverage for crypto makes passage of a law shifting jurisdiction to the CFTC less likely (low impact).
- 2026-02-12 [background] CFE Cboe Ether Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFTC amendment shows regulator activity on crypto futures but does not directly affect whether H.R.3633 will pass and be signed.
- 2026-02-12 [background] CFE Cboe Bitcoin Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFTC approval of a Cboe bitcoin futures market‑maker amendment indicates crypto regulatory activity but is only a weak, indirect signal about CLARITY Act passage.
- 2026-02-03 [background] ICE US New Cryptocurrency Futures Liquidity Provider Program — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-01-29 [background] COIN | Modifications to the Perp Style Futures Market Maker Program — U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2025-12-08 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Announces Launch of Digital Assets Pilot Program for Tokenized Collateral in Derivatives Markets — CFTC pilot demonstrates agency and industry momentum on tokenized assets, modestly increasing odds Congress will codify clarity via the CLARITY Act.

response.text: After the CFTC's late-2025 pilot boosted momentum, 2026 sees the SEC assert sweeping crypto jurisdiction (FR notices, broker-dealer UI guidance), intensifying the turf fight and both raising urgency and dampening prospects for a CFTC-centric CLARITY Act. The CFTC's suit against Minnesota signals federal enforcement may resolve conflicts, easing legislative pressure. Congressional action on H.R.3633 and a presidential signature remain pending in 2026.
